The reason we're here together doing this now is for specifics. You can get the generalities of pitch anything, strong, the grand architecture, these things from the book. Then from the book you can intuit how to do these things. But we're here to get very, very specific and tools to make this happen. Let's get specific about the grand architecture. Step one is you have to build a great landing page for your deal. In our business we call it a pitch deck. What it is, it's some physical or web environment that people can learn about what you have, your deal, your product, your idea, whatever it is you're selling and pitching. Some of you may do that on landing pages on websites. We do it with a PDF called a pitch deck. I'll show you how we do it exactly in a minute. The elements that we have are these. One is an orientation to what the basic deal is. If you sell lawn mowers, in the orientation you have to say it weighs 200 pounds. It has a blade that spins at 20,000 RPM and it can cut a quarter acre on a tank of gas. People will know it's a home-based product. Guys who have a farm with 100 square acres know not to buy this lawn mower. You have to orient people to what it is, who it's for. They don't waste time with you. One is the orientation. Number two is the big idea. You have to give people the big idea. We've done it in the strong series before but I'll review it for you. A big idea can be this. If you're able to build a semiconductor for cell phones such that when you walk into a building, an apartment, or an office complex and you don't lose signal, if you did that then you would have billions of people interested in your product and Verizon, AT&T, and France telecom would want to buy what you have. We in fact have that semiconductor and we've built it into the iPhone and the Blackberry and France telecom and AT&T and Verizon have done contracts with us to buy it. We are working on growing to a billion dollars in revenue with our semiconductor company. That's a simple explanation of the big idea, a semiconductor that doesn't lose signal indoors and selling it to big wireless carriers. You have to give the big idea. In the landing page or the pitch deck you also have to explain why now? Why not do this six months ago? Why wasn't this done two years ago? Why shouldn't the investor or buyer just wait and do it six months from now? Why do it now? Have to explain that. In the landing page or the pitch deck you have to give clear economics. You the buyer pay this much. Over time you make this much back. It costs you this much along the way and this is your return. You have to give clear economics. You have to give a clear value proposition. Who this is for? Why they should buy it? How it is used and how your life will be better after you buy it than it was before. Whether it's a jet fighter or a birthday cake. You have to give the value proposition. We give downside protection. Downside protection is if you buy the deal or the idea or the product how you're protected from losing money. So at least you won't lose money. Or if you do lose money it would just be a little bit and not a lot. Then we always try on the landing page or the pitch deck to give real insight that shows this isn't generic. We know what we're doing. We're inside this market. We know the stuff better than anybody else. If you work with us you can leverage the insight that we have. Let me take you through our pitch deck now and you can see exactly how we do these things. I'll now introduce you to Velocity. This is the method I developed as part of Intersection Capital which is the firm I founded to help companies raise capital and sell themselves. But really its job is to bring as many prospects as possible into phase one of the grand architecture. Again if you recall it's important to have as many people or many groups in phase one to work with so when you get to the closing table you have options. But many people ask how do you get the most amount of people into phase one of the grand architecture process. And I'll show you now what we developed. So this is a company that we're helping and as you can see we've packaged them in a way that captures the big idea in this case 187 words and I'll just go through it. Think for a moment about what happens when you get a wound. You expose the inside of your body to the outside world. What happens next germs, bacteria and other microbes flood in and this is always bad news because once an infection happens your chances of getting very sick get worse minute by minute. Your body will start to fight against the infection your doctor will pitch in to more about this later but there's one issue that could determine 80% of your success. This is the infection within a base of biofilm. Unfortunately named biofilm is not actually good for you it is the foundation for 80% of human wound infections. If you could address the biofilm problem you would have a major opportunity to lower healthcare costs in the US and even save lives. This pitch deck is dedicated to the economic upside from solving the biofilm problem. So unlike an executive summary or a business plan or a PowerPoint presentation what these velocity decks do and I'm going to take you one through one in full is capture the big idea what's the essence of the company. Take you through another one now so you can see this is a very interesting one. We're helping a company who has a completely altruistic purpose and they go in to the most biodiverse ecosystems in the world and try and help the countries they're in save them. Here's the opportunity in 103 words. To save humanity you have to tackle some really nasty problems you already know about them climate change clean energy and species loss. To us the problem of species loss stands out why because it's irreversible. Areas where there is high species concentration have become exceedingly rare and valuable out of 192 countries only 17 make the watch list none are in Europe the US is one the remaining 15 are in South America Central America Africa and Asia all are at risk but many are difficult to reach others are dangerous to operate in once you reach them some are prohibitively expensive to deal with several are targeted by other organizations but there are four countries with areas of diet biodiversity wealth and species concentration we have identified as reachable affordable and saveable or immediate focus is to defend these areas. This prospectus describes one of our priorities how we selected it and why it must be defended and what it costs to do so. So again this velocity deck conveys the big idea what's going on here what's important and what happens if you succeed these are incredibly important ways to help people understand that this deal is worth looking at. I'll show you you know another quick introduction the big idea in 143 words if you want to describe Western North Dakota in just a few words you might say fast growing economy or beautiful country or nice people but if you really wanted to capture what's going on you could simply say no vacancy. Now this pitch deck and these big ideas are available in the resources section and I recommend you look through them. Growing velocity is the way we bring the most amount of people to phase one of our process of the grand architecture. I'd like to show you a little bit more of the Chelexa deck so you can understand beyond the big idea how we show people and get about a deal and get them excited about the deal. So we tried to recover that first of all doesn't offend people and go and makes them say what's inside of this I want to look inside. Here you see a deal summary it's very important to tell people what they're getting here you may not be in the deal business but we say who the CEO is what the product is where they are how much money they've raised so far and what they need the money for and how big their market is. We orient people to the deal so people looking at this can say I'm interested or I'm not interested from these deal basics. Next we go into the big idea I showed you this one already it's very nice what happens when you get a wound you expose the inside of your body to the outside world. Following that we show people right that this is a disruptive technology and it's a big market right it's a big market and how much money is at stake moving forward we show people again quantify the size of the problem. Then we try and show some provocative information and some insight about the market here we say it's the fourth leading cause of death most people don't know that even sophisticated health care providers and many people don't know there are only three options to treating a hospital-gained infection remove it kill it or cover it with dressings. So this is where we start to create some very simple understanding of the product. Next many times if we give information rich pages then we try and provide some eye candy so people don't are not reading facts and information constantly. As we move through this deck we give people a chance to understand what the opportunity to improve on the current state of affairs is moving forward we show people that if you were an investor in this market how you could succeed with it right. Of course we show the value proposition and exactly what the value of this product is we show how the company goes to market and lastly we tell people who the team is and who is going to be executing. This is enough to get many companies interested many people interested in looking at the deal and it fills the pipeline column one phase one the first phase of the grand architecture. I'll take you through one more quickly and then you can explore these yourself you can explore these yourself in the resources section. So again here's nature culture we showed you the big idea to save humanity you have to tackle some really nasty problems as we said before here again you'll start to see it repeated you can orient yourself to the project by the project summary. We give eye candy gives people break from reading factual information then we tell people what the problem is and do a little intrigue it's not what you think after the problem we give the solution following that we show exactly how the business model works a little bit more eye candy right then we like to give a clear definition of exactly what it is. We give some more detail because in this kind of business you have to tell people exactly the locations you're trying to save more eye candy and because this is a business that does pulls at the heartstrings you can show even more eye candy this is one of my favorite pages the Jaguar we show the management team and that's it you have now seen a full velocity deck. Right again this is in the resources section I encourage you to go look at that. My final thoughts on this whether you have a landing page you have a pitch deck or a product brochure it needs to be as compelling as possible to get many many people into phase one of the grand architecture. As you can see super important to build a great pitch deck or landing page to bring as many people into your phase one of your pitch process. That's step one you have to do that. Step two is you have to have a great strong pitch strong set the frame tell the story reveal the intrigue offer the prize nail the hook point get the deal strong you have to have a great pitch you know this is the pitch that takes into account using time frames to your benefit and when you have other people trying to time constrain you you know how to deal with it. Prizing yourself so even though you're the seller and you're offering the deal other people want to get in they're asking to get into your deal I'm the prize you're the commodity you try and win my attention even though I'm the seller you're setting time frames you're using prize frames right you recognize analyst frames when somebody's trying to derail your presentation your pitch by being overly analytical you know how to deal with that close distance on it shut it down you recognize the power frame when people are using their power and their status their authority and their celebrity and their influence and their money to try and get concessions from you to make you economically weak you recognize that that power frame you know how to again close distance on it deal with it shut it down right the strong pitch does all these things right you know how to put your deal in context of changing forces to give your big idea to manage time frames manage attention create tension when people aren't aren't paying quite enough attention to you to put counter pressure on them to make them qualify themselves back to you right strong it does all these things you'll know how to do this you go through it you're in pitch mastery that's step two is to build your great pitch so what this is all about step three you've got to use your pitch deck or your landing page and your great strong pitch together to just attract as many potential deals or buyers or JV partners or investors get attract as many as possible into phase one you're really really going to do that by proliferating the landing page or the pitch deck out in the market as far and as wide as you can get bring it as many people and then use your pitch to get them really into phase one where they want what you have you start working with them. You have to know what your transition actions are between phase one phase two and phase three which is closing I can tell you what ours are they're probably very similar to yours the way it is in your business I'll tell you what ours are in my business to go from phase one to phase two we need what's called an expression of interest sometimes an expression of interest is or I love you and I'm going to do this deal with you sometimes it's a term sheet but it's something that is solid it is either the expression of interest verbally or it is a physical document but it says we plan to advance we don't know if we're going to close and we don't know everything about you we haven't done due diligence yet but based on what we know we like the pricing roughly we like the delivery times roughly we like the value proposition we like the economics we like who you are we want it so moving from phase one to phase two is about wanting for me I can't move somebody from phase one to phase two unless they've expressed interest either verbally or physically in a document and I'm never going to move somebody into phase two out of phase one unless they said I want it so somebody's in phase one and they say yeah I'm kind of interested I'll give you a term sheet that is not enough to move them into phase two I say stop you stay there you cannot advance until you say or and I love you and I am going to do this if it all comes true the things you said you works out and do diligence right move somebody from phase one to phase two you need a a that's a transition point you need something to happen to move people from phase two into phase three now you need something more solid it's not an expression of interest it's a for us it's a purchase and sale agreement for you it might be a draft purchase order might be a contract it might be a very strong verbal agreement all right it might be a letter of interest but it's not the closing document but it's something that that indicates very strong intent to move forward and you also see wanting escalate higher so now we're in phase three which is really just prior to closing to get some from phase three into closing for us we need them to complete all of their due diligence no more questions they've looked at every document they've looked at every technical detail they want to look at they've asked every question no more questions right we're through due diligence and there's a setting of a closing date right no one is allowed to move out of phase three you see my language I allow people to move out of phase three to buy something only when they met my requirements they have completed due diligence no more questions and there's a setting of a closing date now you can come out of phase three and go into closing in a car sale all this might happen by the end of the day right if you sell those giant earth moving caterpillar equipments you know tractors maybe it would be a month to do all this for a screenplay I know some screenwriters and maybe this takes six months or a year phase one phase two phase three might take a year for a screenwriter but he still has to do all these same things get as many people into phase one with the landing page choose what indicates a transition point get people transitioned into phase two get a stronger commitment transition people into phase three pick a closing date and make sure there's no more questions and then get out of phase three into closing so no matter what you have you have to go through this process in an online world phase one is an opt-in right phase two is a live webinar and phase three is the clicking of the order button so we all whether we're online or we sell caterpillar tractors or screenplays right or large companies we all have to go through this grand architecture the focus of this training is the strong pitch but you have to know where you are in the grand architecture in order to just pitch effectively right number one for sure you have to know where you are in the grand architecture to pitch effectively because it's very rare to have somebody who can do a one and done pitch they pitch one person perfectly that person buys and the whole process is over hardly ever happens typically your pitch will get better and better and better over time with the more humans you engage with it right so one is you bring lots of people into the grand architecture so you can pitch many times and get your pitch refined until it's actually working a second reason you need the grand architecture is what we started talking about and the whole reason it actually came into existence you have to eradicate your own neediness no matter how good you are right and I'm pretty damn good right maybe as good as it gets if you have a big deal you're trying to close right there's high stakes and it's likely that you're going to act needy right if you do not have a few options of people to close with right you will act needy not let me make this real for you imagine you're selling a house there's only one buyer that wants to move forward and you go into the final negotiations on price the buyer says we thought about it and we want you to replace the roof right or we won't go forward you want me to replace the roof why because we want you to do it or we're not going to close what do you do right if you're like most people you concede and you lose fifteen thousand dollars or however much that costs however if you have three very motivated buyers and one one of them says hey we want you to replace the roof right what do you do you say go kick rocks not replacing the roof you want a new roof you buy it these other two guys not getting a new roof for free you're not getting it either so decide if you want this house because if you want it with me putting a new roof on it you're not getting it right and you say it that way because you're not needy so in one case you have no other buyers and you say I'll be glad to put a new roof on it for you in the other case you have a couple buyers and you say you don't get a roof if I might just kick you out for being so rude to me never going to get this house the difference between one and the other is running good grand architecture so the third reason you need this and I'm pretty convinced of this it's important to be in a process right when you're doing strong or when you're doing tactics when you're focused on the nitty gritty of pitching and selling and explaining your idea right you are things are constantly changing around you your circumstances are changing and you're adjusting to them right and and you don't know where you are in the larger process the grand architecture gives you a mission you have objectives you have to reach and you have goals now tactics strong and pitching support the mission and the objectives right but you have to know where you are in the big picture right have you ever been deep in a project and gotten that fear in the pit of your stomach that it's not going to be good you're halfway through it and you're like the book isn't going to be good or the design we're making on or the video we're working on or the semiconductor we're designing I'm not sure it's going to be good and or I don't know if it's going to deliver on time or maybe it will be good but it would just be irrelevant nobody will want it anymore right you get this fear like I don't know where I am I'm lost I mean I know I'm doing good work but maybe this work is meaningless in the greater scheme of things and then when your partner is your boss or or or your your you know partner or someone you work with you know who's holding the big picture in their mind who's not down in the weeds with you came in and they reoriented you to the process and why you're doing this thing and how it's certain to be good and reconfirmed with you that you're in the right place at the right time doing the right thing right the grand architecture the way I've designed it is your tool for doing the same thing it's when you get down in the weeds and you get lost it's your way of knowing I'm doing the right thing and everything's going to be okay I'm in good process the grand architecture lets you say to yourself or to you and your partner I am in good process and I don't lose sight of the big picture which is get to the closing table with some options it's important to visualize the grand architecture this is the process you'll be running and you'll of course be using strong when you pitch any individual person or group but you have to understand how to run good process good process is what an investment bank or an investment advisor would do for you and its job is to help create as many closing options as possible for you so you're not needy at the closing table the grand architecture happens in three main phases alright now what you'll do is you'll start with phase one in which you initialize the deal so you will take your deal book or your pitch book as in my business this may be your landing page in your business or even a product brochure and you'll show it to as big an audience as possible and you do that to solicit their interest and some people will become very interested in your deal and your deal will be initialized and you put those people in phase one now this is where you'll use the strong method set the frame tell the story reveal the intrigue offer the prize and start to try and nail the hook point alright so you'll work with these people and pitch them as well as can be done now someone in phase one will say I'm ready to buy and you will say no I'm not going to go outside my process you won't rush them to the closing table because it's likely the closing table if you have no options they will retrade you on price they'll ask for price concessions because they will sense that you have no options so this process is about creating options now you'll use strong to season these people move as many of them as possible up to kind of this closing line alright so you'll rather than letting anybody rush quickly into phase two alright you're going to work with them and get to a point of advancement and a point in advancement is you're going to ask them to signal some desire to move forward and not everyone will move forward some will fall off and you have to be okay with that so you're going to say Bob Mary Susan group I need you to specifically tell me that you're ready to move forward you're interested in the product you're interested in the deal and some will come with you alright so now you'll have a little fewer amount of people to work with but they will be somewhat seasoned at this point you will continue to work with them season them try to advance them in the deal but if anyone tries to say hey I want to rush off and close with you're going to say no that's not my process we're just in phase two of the process so you're going to move them up to the close to the edge here right and you're going to look for an expression of interest in my business that might be a letter of intent a memorandum of understanding or even a contract in your business it might be a purchase order so you'll say hey if you want to go further in this deal at this point alright you need to issue a purchase a draft purchase order or you need to we need to put a draft contract in place and if you don't do that by Friday or by the 15th or in two weeks whatever it is then you're not going to be able to go forward in the deal and you can say that because you have options and if any one of these people say I'm ready to close you're going to say no alright we're not in phase three yet alright and so now you're going to move some of them forward with the expression of interest into phase three not all of them will come with you and now again you're going to continue to season these guys and now they'll be putting pressure on you right they'll be putting pressure on you to give concessions but you're not going to go close any of them prematurely you're going to move them to the edge just as we did in the other phases and now you're going to look for a commitment to close commitment to close and this is what the grand architecture is all about getting to the point where you have options to move into closing so if any one of these individuals or groups says we want to change terms we want to change price we want to price concession you have strength because you have options you say no I'm not giving a price concession and if you don't want to move forward I will close with someone else so now you're going to apply that and you will close with someone now if you sell products you may close with multiple people in the deal business you know typically you'll only have one buyer and this process will allow you to get to the closing process with options and close with one person let's kind of review and take it from the beginning right so if we clear the screen the grand architecture how to create as many closing options as possible we start with a large population we move some of them into phase one right phase one is when we initialize the deal and we have kind of multiple interested parties we don't take any party prematurely to the closing table we bring them up to the edge all right and then we look for a point of advancement all right we get the point of advancement we make them signal their desire to move forward and then move into phase two and phase two we work to season the buyers right and work with them a little bit and again if anyone buyer says I'm ready to close you say we're still in process you move them up to the edge right and you really say now I need another point of advancement or an expression of interest this is something more significant than just a signal of desire we're really looking for some kind of written expression of interest it can be verbal in some businesses but in my business I really want someone to say hey here's a letter of intent here's a draft purchase order right then you're gonna move some of those people they're not into phase three they're not all gonna come with you then we're gonna run the same process over again so you're gonna have less people but they're gonna be very very interested in seasoned in the deal and we use strong to bring them up to the edge they're on the edge and now we really have options going to closing we're gonna ask for commitment to close if any one person tries to retrait us on price or terms we're gonna say no we have other options we're gonna close with someone else and then we're gonna go into closing with one individual that's the grand architecture and it's incredibly important to run this good process when I was raising 15 million dollars for a project near Notre Dame University I had a timeline of 35 days to raise all of the money 15 million dollars ten days into it of ten days of 35 days I had raised one million only one million I started freaking out I was sure it could not be done and I was becoming dejected because I knew if I did not raise 15 million dollars and 35 days we would lose a million dollars of our own money the deposit we put up and now I started going beyond the freak out mode right and and I was going almost to the point of no return where I was scared and scared as needy then my partner John brought me a spreadsheet and was the breakdown of all of our last ten deals and every single one of them tracked to this exact same curve a third of the way in we had only raised a little bit of the money but we always got to the end with all the money so this was my introduction to the grand architecture and its power to calm you down to orient yourself in space and time and to allow you to work with poise and strengthen confidence inside pitch mastery is a grand architecture worksheet you and your business partners will use this to be able to focus on giving great strong pitches right the tactics and keep yourself aware of where you are in the grand process and how to get to the close right without getting needy or nervous or freaking out most importantly though you will know not to be tempted not to be tempted when one prospect in phase one raises his arm and says I'm ready to close because you know that if you rush into closing with that guy he will retrade you at the closing table so when he raises arm prematurely and says I'm ready to close you say sit down you're in my grand architecture you play by my rules you stay in my process and when we're ready we'll let you know when you can buy and what you have to pay all right that's what the grand architecture does for you because if anybody rushes to the closing you know it will be bad for you so you bring everybody along so you have options lets you keep those options right it's the grand architecture that allows you to do this
